sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: The management of esophageal and esophagogastric junction (EGJ) cancers requires a multimodal, multidisciplinary approach. Treatment is guided by histologic subtype (squamous cell carcinoma [SCC] vs. adenocarcinoma), clinical stage (TNM classification), anatomic location (cervical, thoracic, or EGJ), performance status (PS), and biomarker status (HER2, PD-L1, MSI/MMR, CLDN18.2). The primary goal is cure for localized disease and prolongation of survival with palliation for advanced disease. The panel strongly recommends multidisciplinary evaluation for all patients with localized disease. Best supportive care is an integral component of treatment at all stages.

sections.heading: MDT Requirements
sections.content: A multidisciplinary team (MDT) approach is essential for all patients with localized esophagogastric cancer. The panel recommends an infrastructure that encourages joint treatment decision-making by members of all relevant disciplines, including surgical oncology, medical oncology, gastroenterology, radiation oncology, radiology, pathology, nutrition, social work, and palliative care. MDT meetings should occur frequently (weekly or biweekly) to review patient data collectively, develop individualized treatment plans, and ensure optimal sequencing of therapies. The MDT may consider clinical trials when available and applicable.

sections.heading: Performance Status Guide
sections.content: Performance status (PS) is a critical determinant of treatment eligibility and intensity. The Karnofsky Performance Status (KPS) and Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) PS scales are used. Patients with KPS ≥60% or ECOG PS ≤2 are candidates for systemic therapy with or without local therapies. Patients with KPS <60% or ECOG PS ≥3 should be offered best supportive care and/or palliative radiation; systemic therapy is generally not recommended due to potential lack of benefit and increased toxicity. PS should be regularly reassessed to guide ongoing management.

edges.label: Clinically staged I-IVA (except T4b or unresectable N3) disease who are medically fit for potentially curative therapy.
Patients with T4b (unresectable due to invasion of heart, great vessels, trachea) or metastatic (M1) disease, or those who decline or are medically unfit for curative-intent therapy.

cards.title: Curative Intent
cards.detail: Primary treatment options include endoscopic therapy for early-stage (pTis, pT1a, select pT1b) disease; esophagectomy for T1b or greater tumors; and preoperative or definitive chemoradiation, or perioperative chemotherapy (for adenocarcinoma) for locally advanced resectable disease. Treatment decisions are based on precise staging (endoscopic resection, EUS, CT/PET), histologic type, and Siewert classification for EGJ tumors.

cards.title: Palliative Intent
cards.detail: Systemic therapy and/or best supportive care, with local therapies (radiation, endoscopic interventions) for symptom control (e.g., dysphagia, obstruction, bleeding). Treatment is guided by PS, biomarker status (MSI-H/dMMR, HER2, PD-L1, NTRK, RET, BRAF), and patient goals.
